title: WHOOP sync adapter + CLI
description: The end-to-end visible slice: pull WHOOP data and persist it. Add WhoopSyncAdapter (src/medical/whoop/whoop-sync.ts) with sync(window, sink): Promise<IngestionResult>. It does NOT implement IngestionAdapter (that contract is file-path-shaped, ADR-1) — its entry point is a network sync(window). For each collection in [recovery, sleep, cycle, workout] it loops WhoopClient.fetchPage following the cursor, maps each WhoopRecord to HealthObservation[] (source='whoop', a fixed metric name + fixed unit per collection field; tStart from the record's start ISO; id left to the store's content-derived SHA-256), and writes via the EXISTING ObservationSink.writeBatch in bounded batches (reuse StoreObservationSink + HealthDataStore.upsertObservations unchanged). Resume is idempotent: re-running over an overlapping window inserts only genuinely-new rows via INSERT OR IGNORE; a mid-pagination failure aborts with committed batches intact and never partially corrupts the store (fail-closed, never fail-open). Add the CLI: `bober medical whoop sync [--since <iso>]` in src/cli/commands/medical.ts mirroring the existing `medical import` command (src/cli/commands/medical.ts:30-72): build EgressGuard.fromConfig + WhoopTokenStore + WhoopClient + WhoopSyncAdapter + StoreObservationSink + HealthDataStore, call egress.assertAllowed('device-connection') (surfacing a clear 'not enabled' message), run the sync over the window (default last 7 days or --since), print the IngestionResult {recordsParsed, newRows}, store.close() in finally, set process.exitCode on error and NEVER throw.

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build) and zero typecheck errors (npm run typecheck).

WhoopSyncAdapter.sync(window, sink) pulls all four collections via WhoopClient (injected, fixture-backed), maps records to HealthObservation rows with source='whoop' and a fixed metric+unit per mapped field, writes them through ObservationSink.writeBatch in bounded batches, and returns IngestionResult {recordsParsed, newRows}. A unit test with a fixture WhoopClient and an in-temp-dir HealthDataStore asserts rows land with source='whoop' and the counts are correct.

Idempotent resume: running sync twice over the same/overlapping window yields newRows>0 on the first run and newRows===0 on the second (INSERT OR IGNORE on the content-derived SHA-256 dedup key), with no duplicate observations in the store. A unit test asserts the second run adds zero rows.

Partial-failure safety: when the fixture WhoopClient throws mid-pagination (after >=1 committed batch), sync rejects/aborts, the already-committed batch rows remain valid in the store, no partial/corrupt row is written, and a subsequent successful re-run reaches the same end-state. A unit test asserts committed rows survive and a re-run completes.

`bober medical whoop sync` with the device-connection axis OFF prints a clear 'device-connection egress not enabled' message and exits with process.exitCode 1 without throwing and without constructing a WhoopClient that performs HTTP. A test invoking the command action asserts the message and exit code.

With the axis on but WHOOP_CLIENT_ID/SECRET unset, or with no stored refresh token, the command prints a clear 'set WHOOP_CLIENT_ID/WHOOP_CLIENT_SECRET' or 'authorize first' message and exits with process.exitCode 1 (never throws). A test asserts each branch.

The full test suite passes (npm run test) with no regression and the scoped ESLint medical boundary still passes (npm run lint) — whoop-sync.ts and the CLI contain no network imports (all HTTP remains in whoop-client.ts).

On a successful sync the command prints recordsParsed and newRows and calls HealthDataStore.close() in a finally block (no leaked DB handle); ingestion writes an audit entry event:'ingest' (IDs/enums only) consistent with the existing import path. A test asserts the printed result and the audit entry.

nonGoals: Do not add a persisted sync cursor / checkpoint (Open Question; rely on idempotent re-run, ADR-4).
Do not add UUID-keyed upsert or in-place-mutation handling (Open Question; content-derived dedup only).
Do not add new numerics primitives for WHOOP metrics — reuse the existing closed whitelist.
Do not modify the IngestionAdapter interface, ObservationSink, or the health_observations schema.
Do not add webhooks; sync is on-demand only.
stopConditions: `bober medical whoop sync` persists WHOOP observations end-to-end with deterministic idempotent dedup, fails closed on partial errors, surfaces clear axis-off/credential errors with exit code 1 and never throws, all HTTP stays in whoop-client.ts, and build/typecheck/test/lint are green with no regression
definitionOfDone: src/medical/whoop/whoop-sync.ts exports WhoopSyncAdapter { readonly source:'whoop'; sync(window, sink): Promise<IngestionResult> } that pages WhoopClient across the four collections, maps records to HealthObservation[] (source='whoop'), writes via the existing ObservationSink in bounded batches, is idempotent on re-run, and aborts cleanly without partial corruption on error. src/cli/commands/medical.ts gains a `whoop` subcommand with a `sync [--since <iso>]` action mirroring `medical import`: assertAllowed('device-connection'), wire the pieces, print IngestionResult, store.close() in finally, set process.exitCode on error and never throw. Collocated tests (fixture WhoopClient + temp-dir store) cover mapping/counts, idempotent resume, partial-failure safety, and the CLI axis-off/credential-missing/success branches. Build, typecheck, test, lint pass with no regression; no network import outside whoop-client.ts.
assumptions: StoreObservationSink and HealthDataStore.upsertObservations exist and are reused unchanged (src/medical/ingestion.ts:16-32, src/medical/health-store.ts:155-175); WhoopSyncAdapter depends on the ObservationSink interface, not a concrete store.
IngestionResult is the existing {recordsParsed, newRows} shape (src/medical/types.ts:191-194); recordsParsed counts mapped observations seen, newRows the count returned by upsertObservations.

The CLI command tree exposes registerMedicalCommand (src/cli/commands/medical.ts) where a `.command("whoop").command("sync")` is added mirroring `medical import`; the import action's error/exitCode/finally pattern (medical.ts:69-73) is the template.

A default sync window of the last 7 days is used when --since is omitted; the window start/end are computed from an injected now (or Date in the CLI boundary only) and passed as ISO strings to sync — the store and adapter never read the clock.

Each WHOOP collection maps to one or more fixed (metric, unit) pairs decided in code (e.g. recovery score, resting heart rate, HRV, sleep duration); the mapping is a small reviewable table, and unmapped fields are skipped rather than guessed.
generatorNotes:
Read FIRST: src/medical/whoop/whoop-client.ts + whoop-token.ts (from Sprint 2 — the sync depends on these), src/medical/ingestion.ts (IngestionNormalizer, ObservationSink, StoreObservationSink, bounded-batch backpressure pattern), src/medical/health-store.ts:32-42,155-175 (content-derived SHA-256 id + upsertObservations INSERT OR IGNORE returning new-row count), src/medical/adapters/apple-health.ts:13,96-99 (bounded ~1000-row batch + writeBatch backpressure precedent), src/cli/commands/medical.ts:30-72 (the `medical import` command shape: action, print, store.close() in finally, process.exitCode on error, never throw), src/medical/types.ts (IngestionResult :191-194, HealthObservation :96-107, AuditEvent 'ingest').

whoop-sync.ts (NO network import — it depends on WhoopClient, which owns HTTP): class WhoopSyncAdapter { readonly source='whoop'; constructor(private client: WhoopClient) {} async sync(window: SyncWindow, sink: ObservationSink): Promise<IngestionResult> }. For each collection in ['recovery','sleep','cycle','workout']: cursor=undefined; do { page = await client.fetchPage(collection, window, cursor); const obs = mapWhoopRecords(collection, page.records); accumulate recordsParsed += obs.length; await sink.writeBatch(obs, []); cursor = page.nextCursor } while (cursor). Sum newRows from the sink/store path. mapWhoopRecords: a small fixed table mapping known WHOOP fields to (metric, unit) with source='whoop', tStart=record.tStartIso, value=number; skip fields not in the table. Let the store derive id (content-derived) — do NOT set id from the WHOOP UUID. On any fetch/throw, let it propagate (committed batches already persisted via per-batch transaction) — do NOT catch-and-continue (fail-closed).

CLI: in registerMedicalCommand add medicalCmd.command('whoop').command('sync').option('--since <iso>') with an action that: loads config, builds EgressGuard.fromConfig(config); wrap the body in try/catch; call egress.assertAllowed('device-connection') (catch -> print 'device-connection egress not enabled — set medical.egress.deviceConnection' and set process.exitCode=1, return); build WhoopTokenStore (clientCredentials may throw 'set WHOOP_CLIENT_ID/SECRET' -> catch, message, exit 1); if readRefreshToken() is undefined -> print 'authorize first', exit 1; build WhoopClient + WhoopSyncAdapter + HealthDataStore + StoreObservationSink; compute window (--since or now-7d) as ISO; result = await adapter.sync(window, sink); print recordsParsed/newRows; in finally store.close(). Mirror the exact error/exitCode discipline of `medical import` (medical.ts:69-73) — never throw out of the action. Append an 'ingest' audit entry consistent with the existing import path if that path does so.

Tests: whoop-sync.test.ts with a fake WhoopClient returning fixture pages + a real HealthDataStore in a temp dir: assert rows land with source='whoop' and counts; run twice -> second newRows===0; a fake client that throws on page 2 -> assert page-1 rows persisted, sync rejects, re-run completes. CLI test: invoke the action with axis off (assert message + exitCode 1, no HTTP), with env unset (assert message + exit 1), with axis on + fixture client (assert printed recordsParsed/newRows + store closed + 'ingest' audit entry). Inject FetchLike/now so no real network or clock. ESM/.js extensions, unicode section headers, small single-purpose files.
evaluatorNotes:
sc-3-2: fixture WhoopClient returns known records across the four collections; after sync, query HealthDataStore for source='whoop' rows and assert metric/unit/tStart mapping + recordsParsed/newRows.
sc-3-3: run sync twice over the same window; assert first newRows>0, second newRows===0, and the row count in the store is unchanged after the second run (no duplicates).
sc-3-4: fixture client commits page 1 then throws on page 2; assert sync rejects, page-1 rows are present and well-formed (no half-written row), then a clean re-run reaches full expected state.
sc-3-5: invoke the CLI action with deviceConnection false; assert the printed 'not enabled' message, process.exitCode===1, no throw, and that no fetch/HTTP occurred (spy FetchLike never constructed/called).
sc-3-6: axis on, env unset -> assert 'set WHOOP_CLIENT_ID/WHOOP_CLIENT_SECRET' + exit 1; axis on, creds set, no refresh token -> assert 'authorize first' + exit 1.
sc-3-7: npm run lint clean; grep whoop-sync.ts and the CLI diff for network imports/fetch -> expect none (all HTTP in whoop-client.ts); run the FULL suite for no regression.
sc-3-8: on a successful fixture sync assert stdout contains recordsParsed and newRows, store.close() was called (no open-handle warning / spy on close), and an event:'ingest' audit entry (IDs/enums only) was appended.
Build/typecheck/test/lint all green.
